tools.name: create_qr_code
tools.description: Create a new QR code. Authenticated users (API key required) get a permanent short URL that can be retargeted at any time without reprinting the physical QR image — limits are set by the account plan. Anonymous users receive a one-time data URI image that is not stored and cannot be updated. On succ

tools.name: get_qr_code
tools.description: Look up a single QR code by its internal ID or short code. Returns a pre-formatted markdown table with fields: Title, ID, Short Code, Short URL, Destination, Status, Created, QR Code Link. Display the table exactly as received — do not reformat or summarise. Requires authentication.

tools.name: list_qr_codes
tools.description: List all QR codes in your account. Returns one pre-formatted markdown table per QR code with fields: Title, ID, Short Code, Short URL, Destination, Status, Created, QR Code Link. Display each table exactly as received — do not reformat or summarise. Requires authentication.

tools.name: get_qr_scans
tools.description: Get the total lifetime scan count for a QR code. Returns a pre-formatted markdown table with fields: Title, ID, Short Code, Short URL, Destination, Status, Created, QR Code Link, Total Scans. Display the table exactly as received — do not reformat or summarise. Requires authentication.

tools.name: update_qr_target
tools.description: Change the destination URL of a QR code without touching the printed image. Print once, redirect anywhere — forever. Accepts either the internal qr_code_id or the short_code from the redirect URL. On success the result is a pre-formatted markdown table — display it exactly as received, do not reform

tools.name: delete_qr_code
tools.description: Permanently delete a QR code and its scan history. This action cannot be undone. To prevent accidental or injected deletions, you MUST supply confirm_title — the exact title of the code as returned by get_qr_code or list_qr_codes. If the title does not match the stored record, the deletion is refuse

tools.name: get_account_info
tools.description: Get your account details: current plan, QR code usage, and which features are available. Use this before create_qr_code to check remaining capacity, or to confirm which features are active on your plan. Requires authentication.

tools.name: request_api_key
tools.description: Get a free API token — no account or email required. The token gives you up to 5 QR codes that can be updated at any time. IMPORTANT LIFETIME WARNING: the token expires after 90 days, and QR codes are permanently deleted ~90 days after that (~180 days total). To keep codes alive permanently, claim a

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "delete_qr_code" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Permanently delete a QR code and its scan history. This action cannot be undone. To prevent accidental or injected deletions, you MUST supply confirm_title — th…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "request_api_key" references sensitive artifacts (authorization, bearer). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Get a free API token — no account or email required. The token gives you up to 5 QR codes that can be updated at any time. IMPORTANT LIFETIME WARNING: the token…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "request_api_key" stacks urgency/override words (important, warning). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Get a free API token — no account or email required. The token gives you up to 5 QR codes that can be updated at any time. IMPORTANT LIFETIME WARNING: the token…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: create_qr_code, get_qr_code, list_qr_codes, get_qr_scans, request_api_key · Private data: create_qr_code, delete_qr_code, request_api_key · Public sink: request_api_key · Destructive: delete_qr_code.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: create_qr_code, get_qr_code, list_qr_codes, get_qr_scans, request_api_key · Private data: create_qr_code, delete_qr_code, request_api_key · Public sink: request_api_key · Destructive: delete_qr_code

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
